mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth accelerates, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity valuation risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when CPIAUCSL breaches the threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below zero in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic growth that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals synchronized supply and labor market strength, creating a favorable risk environment that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate models.
